([Urdu](/wiki/Urdu_language "Urdu language"): بابر جاوید) is a [Pakistani](/wiki/Pakistani "Pakistani") television and film producer and director. He has produced and directed series such as *[Meri Zaat Zarra-e-Benishan](/wiki/Meri_Zaat_Zarra-e-Benishan "Meri Zaat Zarra-e-Benishan")*, *[Qaid-e-Tanhai](/wiki/Qaid-e-Tanhai "Qaid-e-Tanhai")*, *[Mera Saaein](/wiki/Mera_Saaein "Mera Saaein")*, *[Main Abdul Qadir Hoon](/wiki/Main_Abdul_Qadir_Hoon "Main Abdul Qadir Hoon")*, *[Aasmanon Pay Likha](/wiki/Aasmanon_Pay_Likha "Aasmanon Pay Likha")*, *[Bashar Momin](/wiki/Bashar_Momin "Bashar Momin")*, *[Adhoora Bandhan](/wiki/Adhoora_Bandhan "Adhoora Bandhan")*, and *[Mor Mahal](/wiki/Mor_Mahal "Mor Mahal")*,[[1]](#cite_note-:0-1) all under his production company [A&B Entertainment](/wiki/A%26B_Entertainment "A&B Entertainment") that he co-founded with actor turned filmmaker [Asif Raza Mir](/wiki/Asif_Raza_Mir "Asif Raza Mir").[[2]](#cite_note-2) Javed has earned eight [Lux Style Awards](/wiki/Lux_Style_Awards "Lux Style Awards") as a director and producer. In 2016, he received his first [ARY Film Award](/wiki/ARY_Film_Awards "ARY Film Awards") nomination for the producing 2015 biopic film *[Manto](/wiki/Manto_(2015_film) "Manto (2015 film)")*.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Pakistani producer and director

| Babar Javed | |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Born | Babar Javed Mughal   [Karachi](/wiki/Karachi "Karachi"), [Sindh](/wiki/Sindh "Sindh"), [Pakistan](/wiki/Pakistan "Pakistan") |
| Education | Diploma in Production/Direction and TV Journalism from Asian Academy of Film & Television AAFT (India)   Diploma in Film School from London Academy of Radio, Film and TV (UK)   Director's course in Cinematography from Brighton School |
| Occupations | [film producer](/wiki/Film_producer "Film producer"), [television producer](/wiki/Television_producer "Television producer"), [television director](/wiki/Television_director "Television director"), concept creator Production house [A&B Entertainment](/wiki/A%26B_Entertainment "A&B Entertainment") [GEO Entertainment](/wiki/GEO_Entertainment "GEO Entertainment"), [GEO Kahani](/wiki/GEO_Kahani "GEO Kahani"), BJ Productions, and Big Magic |
| Years active | 2001-present |

**Babar Javed** ([Urdu](/wiki/Urdu_language "Urdu language"): بابر جاوید) is a [Pakistani](/wiki/Pakistani "Pakistani") television and film producer and director. He has produced and directed series such as *[Meri Zaat Zarra-e-Benishan](/wiki/Meri_Zaat_Zarra-e-Benishan "Meri Zaat Zarra-e-Benishan")*, *[Qaid-e-Tanhai](/wiki/Qaid-e-Tanhai "Qaid-e-Tanhai")*, *[Mera Saaein](/wiki/Mera_Saaein "Mera Saaein")*, *[Main Abdul Qadir Hoon](/wiki/Main_Abdul_Qadir_Hoon "Main Abdul Qadir Hoon")*, *[Aasmanon Pay Likha](/wiki/Aasmanon_Pay_Likha "Aasmanon Pay Likha")*, *[Bashar Momin](/wiki/Bashar_Momin "Bashar Momin")*, *[Adhoora Bandhan](/wiki/Adhoora_Bandhan "Adhoora Bandhan")*, and *[Mor Mahal](/wiki/Mor_Mahal "Mor Mahal")*,[[1]](#cite_note-:0-1) all under his production company [A&B Entertainment](/wiki/A%26B_Entertainment "A&B Entertainment") that he co-founded with actor turned filmmaker [Asif Raza Mir](/wiki/Asif_Raza_Mir "Asif Raza Mir").[[2]](#cite_note-2) Javed has earned eight [Lux Style Awards](/wiki/Lux_Style_Awards "Lux Style Awards") as a director and producer. In 2016, he received his first [ARY Film Award](/wiki/ARY_Film_Awards "ARY Film Awards") nomination for the producing 2015 biopic film *[Manto](/wiki/Manto_(2015_film) "Manto (2015 film)")*.

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Babar_Javed&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Career")]

Javed started his career in 2001 as a director and producer, and later in 2009, Babar co-founded a production company [A&B Entertainment](/wiki/A%26B_Entertainment "A&B Entertainment") with actor [Asif Raza Mir](/wiki/Asif_Raza_Mir "Asif Raza Mir") and produced projects for [Hum TV](/wiki/Hum_TV "Hum TV"), [ARY Digital](/wiki/ARY_Digital "ARY Digital"), and [Geo TV](/wiki/Geo_TV "Geo TV"). Their debut project, *Diya Jalay*, starring [Samiya Mumtaz](/wiki/Samiya_Mumtaz "Samiya Mumtaz"), earned them critical praise, and their claim to fame came with the 2010 ensemble cast drama serial also starring Mumtaz, *[Meri Zaat Zarra-e-Benishan](/wiki/Meri_Zaat_Zarra-e-Benishan "Meri Zaat Zarra-e-Benishan")*, by [Umera Ahmed](/wiki/Umera_Ahmed "Umera Ahmed"), that earned the duo as producers recognition and positive appraisal.[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4) In 2013, the duo merged their production company with [Geo TV](/wiki/Geo_TV "Geo TV") for producing and directing the channel's projects only, prior to the merger they usually created projects for [ARY Digital](/wiki/ARY_Digital "ARY Digital") and [Geo Entertainment](/wiki/Geo_Entertainment "Geo Entertainment").[[5]](#cite_note-5)
